subject: Geology
title: Special Mineral Properties
description: A quick win: understand Special Mineral Properties.

hook.text: Some minerals glow under UV light. Others fizz in acid. A few are magnetic. These quirks clinch identification.

buildup.text: Fluorescence, magnetism, acid reaction, taste, and smell are special properties unique to certain minerals.

discovery.text: Calcite fizzes in hydrochloric acid. Magnetite sticks to magnets. Halite tastes salty. Sulfur smells like eggs.

twist.text: These unusual tests work when standard methods fail. Sometimes tasting or smelling a mineral is the fastest answer.

climax.text: Mineral identification uses all senses and simple chemistry. The best geologists use every clue available.

punchline.text: Sometimes you have to lick the rock.

quiz.question: Which mineral fizzes when acid is applied?
